But like the sort of way that I frame what's happened here is that the LLNs have basically expanded the TAM of search by like 10x.
And that hasn't fully materialized yet in the numbers, but I think it will.
And the way that it's done that is by way of expanding the type of the number of queries that you can now ask that you would have never asked before, because rather than being limited to four key.
And looking at 10 blue links, you can now just type a whole  paragraph, and it will figure it out.
And so I think the sort of TAM for extremely long tail content just went from effectively zero to not effectively zero, and you just have to produce a lot of long tail content.
And I think in a very meaningful way, the only way to do that is with LLMs, which is funny.
It's like you have to use that because otherwise you can't actually afford to produce that like insanely nuanced long tail content for, you know, the five users a year who will see it.
